solve_notes: Added a `debug` boolean field to `Retrofit` and a `debug(boolean)` method to `Retrofit.Builder`. In `nextResponseBodyConverter()`, when `debug=true`, each factory's class name and whether it returned a converter (or null) is logged to stdout. The debug flag flows through the constructor and Builder copy constructor following the same pattern as `validateEagerly`.
task_text:
When `Retrofit.responseBodyConverter()` iterates through registered
`Converter.Factory` instances in `Retrofit.java`, there is no
visibility into which factories were tried and why they returned
`null`. Add a logging mode (enabled via `Retrofit.Builder.debug(true)`)
that logs each factory's name and whether it provided a converter,
helping developers diagnose converter registration issues.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the three edited definitions. The thrash_preventing set adds validateEagerly (the template for how boolean flags are wired end-to-end) and the Retrofit constructor (to avoid accidentally missing a constructor parameter). Without these an agent would have to search for the pattern itself.
